task_description: Screen each abstract for relevance to: deep learning applied to retinal fundus image analysis for eye disease detection or grading. Label RELEVANT if the paper applies deep learning to retinal fundus photographs or OCT images to detect or grade any ocular condition. Label NOT_RELEVANT if the imaging modality, method type, or clinical task does not satisfy these criteria.

papers.title: Transfer Learning from ImageNet for Diabetic Retinopathy Grading in Low-Resource Settings
papers.abstract: High-quality retinal grading AI typically requires large labelled datasets unavailable in low-income settings. We study transfer learning from ImageNet-pretrained ResNet-50 fine-tuned on 4,800 fundus photographs from two African hospital systems. Quadratic weighted kappa 0.82 is achieved on a held-out validation set, comparable to models trained on ten times more data. We provide a practical guide for deployment on low-cost tablet fundus cameras.
papers.topic_hint: transfer learning, diabetic retinopathy, fundus photography, low-resource, ResNet
papers.methodology_hint: ResNet-50 transfer learning, 4800 fundus images, kappa 0.82, low-resource deployment
papers.claimed_contribution: ResNet-50 transfer learning for DR grading with kappa 0.82 in low-resource settings, deployment guide.

papers.title: Reinforcement Learning for Surgical Instrument Tracking in Robot-Assisted Operations
papers.abstract: Accurate instrument tracking is critical for autonomous robotic surgery. We train a reinforcement learning agent using a combination of surgical video frames and kinematic sensor streams from 320 laparoscopic procedures. The agent achieves 94% tracking accuracy at 30 fps, outperforming optical flow and convolutional tracker baselines. No fundus imaging, retinal analysis, or ocular disease detection is involved.
papers.topic_hint: reinforcement learning, surgical robotics, instrument tracking, laparoscopic
papers.methodology_hint: RL, surgical video, kinematic sensors, tracking accuracy 94%, surgical setting — no fundus imaging
papers.claimed_contribution: RL agent for surgical instrument tracking at 94% accuracy, 30 fps, in laparoscopic procedures.

papers.title: Attention-Guided CNN for Age-Related Macular Degeneration Detection from Fundus Images
papers.abstract: Age-related macular degeneration is a leading cause of irreversible vision loss. We develop an attention-guided convolutional network trained on 58,000 fundus photographs from the UK Biobank to detect and grade AMD severity. The model achieves macro-AUC 0.95 across four AMD stages and correctly localises drusen and geographic atrophy using spatial attention maps. Performance is maintained across three different fundus camera models.
papers.topic_hint: CNN, AMD detection, fundus photography, UK Biobank, attention maps
papers.methodology_hint: attention CNN, 58k fundus images, UK Biobank, macro-AUC 0.95, multi-camera validation
papers.claimed_contribution: Attention CNN for AMD grading from fundus images, macro-AUC 0.95 with drusen localisation across cameras.

papers.title: Contrastive Self-Supervised Learning for Chest X-Ray Abnormality Detection
papers.abstract: We apply contrastive self-supervised pre-training on 1.3 million unlabelled chest radiographs and fine-tune for 14 pathology labels from the CheXpert benchmark. AUC 0.88 averaged across pathologies is achieved with 100 labelled examples per class, demonstrating strong label efficiency. This work targets thoracic imaging, not retinal fundus analysis.
papers.topic_hint: contrastive learning, chest X-ray, self-supervised, CheXpert, thoracic pathology
papers.methodology_hint: contrastive pre-training, chest X-ray, AUC 0.88, label-efficient — chest not fundus imaging
papers.claimed_contribution: Contrastive pre-training for chest X-ray pathology, AUC 0.88 with 100 labels per class.

papers.title: Sparse Transformer for Glaucoma Suspect Classification from Optic Disc OCT Volumes
papers.abstract: Glaucoma suspects — patients with elevated intraocular pressure but no confirmed field loss — are challenging to monitor. We train a sparse transformer on 3D optical coherence tomography volumes of the optic disc from 14,000 suspect patients. Longitudinal OCT sequences predict conversion to glaucoma within two years with AUC 0.86, outperforming retinal nerve fibre layer thickness thresholds. Code and model weights are released.
papers.topic_hint: sparse transformer, glaucoma suspect, OCT, optic disc, progression prediction
papers.methodology_hint: sparse transformer, 3D OCT, 14k patients, AUC 0.86, RNFL thickness comparison, public code
papers.claimed_contribution: Sparse transformer for glaucoma conversion prediction from longitudinal OCT, AUC 0.86.

papers.title: U-Net Variants for Prostate Cancer Segmentation in Multiparametric MRI
papers.abstract: We systematically evaluate five U-Net architectural variants for prostate cancer lesion segmentation in T2-weighted and diffusion-weighted MRI from 320 patients. The best variant achieves Dice 0.72 on clinically significant lesions. Attention gates and deep supervision provide the largest gains. This work involves pelvic MRI, not retinal or optic imaging.
papers.topic_hint: U-Net, prostate cancer, MRI segmentation, multiparametric, lesion detection
papers.methodology_hint: U-Net variants, T2+DWI MRI, Dice 0.72, prostate lesion — pelvic not retinal imaging
papers.claimed_contribution: U-Net variant comparison for prostate cancer segmentation, Dice 0.72, best with attention and deep supervision.

papers.title: EfficientNet for Retinal Vessel Segmentation and Diabetic Retinopathy Screening
papers.abstract: Retinal vessel morphology provides diagnostic information beyond manual grading. We train an EfficientNet-B3 encoder-decoder on DRIVE and CHASE_DB1 benchmarks for vessel segmentation, achieving Dice 0.82. We further fine-tune the vessel representation for diabetic retinopathy screening, using vessel tortuosity and calibre as auxiliary features that improve DR detection F1 from 0.81 to 0.87 on a held-out validation set.
papers.topic_hint: EfficientNet, retinal vessel segmentation, DRIVE, diabetic retinopathy, fundus
papers.methodology_hint: EfficientNet-B3, DRIVE/CHASE_DB1, vessel Dice 0.82, DR F1 0.87 with vessel features
papers.claimed_contribution: EfficientNet retinal vessel segmentation (Dice 0.82) improving DR screening F1 to 0.87 via vessel features.

papers.title: Vision Transformer for Histopathology Grading of Colorectal Cancer Biopsies
papers.abstract: We fine-tune a ViT-L/16 on 90,000 H&E stained colorectal biopsy whole-slide images for five-tier cancer grading. Macro-F1 0.91 is achieved, outperforming ResNet and attention-pooling baselines. Patch-level attention visualisations match pathologist annotations on 87% of cases. No retinal, fundus, or ophthalmic imaging is involved.
papers.topic_hint: ViT, histopathology, colorectal cancer grading, whole-slide images, H&E
papers.methodology_hint: ViT-L/16, 90k WSIs, macro-F1 0.91, pathologist comparison — histopathology not fundus
papers.claimed_contribution: ViT for colorectal cancer grading from H&E WSIs, macro-F1 0.91, pathologist-concordant attention.

papers.title: Semi-Supervised Deep Learning for Retinal Arteriolar-to-Venular Ratio Measurement
papers.abstract: Arteriolar-to-venular ratio (AVR) from fundus photographs is a biomarker for hypertension and cardiovascular risk. Manual AVR measurement is time-consuming. We develop a semi-supervised deep learning pipeline trained on 2,000 labelled and 30,000 unlabelled fundus images to automatically segment arteries and veins and compute AVR. Agreement with expert measurement achieves intraclass correlation 0.89, enabling population-scale cardiovascular screening.
papers.topic_hint: semi-supervised deep learning, fundus photography, AVR measurement, vessel segmentation
papers.methodology_hint: semi-supervised DL, 2k labelled + 30k unlabelled fundus, ICC 0.89, AVR computation
papers.claimed_contribution: Semi-supervised deep learning for AVR from fundus images, ICC 0.89 with expert measurement.

papers.title: Federated Learning for Skin Lesion Classification Across Dermatology Clinics
papers.abstract: Dermatology AI benefits from multi-site diversity but patient data cannot be centralised. We apply federated learning to train a skin lesion classifier across 18 dermatology clinics without sharing images. AUC 0.91 for melanoma detection is achieved, matching centralised training. The federated model generalises better to under-represented skin tones than single-site models. No retinal or fundus imaging is involved.
papers.topic_hint: federated learning, skin lesion, dermatology, melanoma detection, multi-site
papers.methodology_hint: federated learning, 18 clinics, AUC 0.91, melanoma — skin dermatology not fundus
papers.claimed_contribution: Federated melanoma detector AUC 0.91 matching centralised, better generalisation across skin tones.
